name: Analysis of Variance (ANOVA)
applicable_scenarios: compare means across three or more groups
one-way and factorial designs for experimental data
partition total variance into between-group and within-group components
typical_cases: agricultural yield across three fertilizer treatments
reaction time under four drug doses
manufacturing defect rates across multiple shifts
common_pitfalls: assumes homogeneity of variance (Levene/Bartlett); otherwise use Welch ANOVA
ANOVA only tells you means differ, not which pair — follow with Tukey HSD
non-normal residuals with small n mislead F; consider Kruskal-Wallis
unbalanced designs require Type II/III sums of squares
